title: Non-record: TrigramHash — iso-parametric bigram(96)+trigram(32), val_bpb=1.5275 (1xH100)
body:
## Summary

Non-record submission exploring whether 3-token co-occurrence patterns add signal beyond the current BigramHash.

**Tested on**: 1×H100 (not competition-standard 8×H100)

## Approach

Iso-parametric split of SOTA `BigramHash(10240, dim=128)` into:

| Component | Params |
|-----------|--------|
| BigramHash(10240, dim=96) | 983,040 |
| TrigramHash(10240, dim=32) | 327,680 |
| **Total** | **1,310,720** (identical to SOTA) |

Hash function: `(36313*t[i] XOR 27191*t[i-1] XOR 18731*t[i-2]) % (vocab_size - 1)`

Three independent prime multipliers ensure each token position contributes orthogonal bit patterns. Positions 0 and 1 fall back to a padding bucket (same convention as bigram for position 0).

## Result

- **val_bpb**: 1.5275 (1×H100, 871 steps, 600s wallclock)
- **Artifact**: 15,421,256 bytes with zstd-22 ✓
- **Base**: `2026-03-20_10L_Int5MLP_MuonWD04_SWA50` (1.14276 BPB)

The 1×H100 result uses ~8× fewer training steps than an 8×H100 run in the same wallclock budget, so the score is not directly comparable to SOTA. Architecture is novel, artifact-compliant, and runs cleanly.

## Notes

The iso-parametric constraint is exact: total embedding parameters unchanged vs SOTA. Artifact size with zstd-22 compression is 15.4MB, within the 16MB limit. All other hyperparameters identical to the SOTA baseline.
